signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7034 — USD (Leader) vs AUD (Laggard) spread hits 1.618% as hawkish Fed concerns and war-driven US PPI fuel a dollar rally. AUD COT LF net long +56,800 (76.5% long) faces liquidation risk as negative CESI (-9 misses) confirms deteriorating fundamentals. Volatility premium is extreme with VRP at +10.4% (IV 18.7% vs HV30 8.3%), signaling heavy institutional hedging against further AUD downside. 60bp AUD carry advantage is insufficient to offset the G10 underperformance theme.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Watch + Carry + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.07 — Price holds above the 160.00 psychological level previously associated with BoJ verbal warnings. Despite 287bp OIS carry favoring USD, 25d RR at -1.09 (moderate put skew) confirms the options market is paying for JPY strength/downside protection. Retail sentiment is 79% short, diverging from the massive COT LF net short of -105,136; retail is fading the institutional carry trade at the intervention trigger. Sustained trade above 160.00 increases the risk of direct BoJ action.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail vs Institutional Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5821 — Retail sentiment is 91% long, directly contradicting the institutional COT LF net short of -27,797 (only 15.1% long). This extreme divergence at the 0.5800 handle signals high risk of a retail capitulation flush if USD strength persists. NZD real rate of -0.83% remains the most accommodative in the G10, providing no structural support against the 150bp USD carry advantage.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Policy Dilemma + Vol Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1565 — ECB raised rates today but signaled a potential July pause if energy prices stabilize, creating a 'dovish hike' narrative. EUR COT LF net short -22,320 aligns with the 175bp USD carry advantage. VRP at +2.8% (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.3%) indicates the market is pricing in higher volatility than realized as the ECB path becomes less clear. Watch for a break of 1.1500 if energy-driven inflation concerns subside.
